Reports
Contact

Contact I Ching Reading Guide.

Use this page for reader questions, correction notes, partnership inquiries, and paid report support.

Email:
guan@shanyuegroup.com

What to include

If you are asking about a paid report, include the report link, order ID if available, the email address used at checkout, and a short description of the issue. Do not send sensitive medical, legal, financial, identity, or private relationship records. The site can help with delivery and general reading context, but it cannot provide professional advice.

If you are reporting a content issue, include the page URL and the sentence that seems unclear or inaccurate. I Ching terminology can vary by translation, so useful correction notes should explain whether the issue is wording, cultural context, broken navigation, or a factual mistake.

For partnership or product inquiries, include the site, product type, audience, and whether the request is about books, study cards, learning tools, cultural education, or report delivery. The site is selective about commercial placement and avoids products that make exaggerated spiritual, medical, or financial claims.

Email inquiry

This form is a convenience layout. The email button opens your mail app so you can send the message directly.

Contact use

When to send a message

Send a message if you need help with a report delivery link, notice a broken page, want to suggest a clearer explanation, or have a partnership inquiry that fits the site. The strongest messages include a page URL, a short description of the issue, and enough context to reproduce the problem. For paid report support, include the email address used at checkout and the report link if you have it.

Do not send private medical records, legal documents, financial account details, passwords, or sensitive identity information. The contact channel is not built for crisis support or professional case review. If the matter is urgent, risky, or legally important, contact the appropriate professional service first.

Support expectations

What happens after contact

Most support messages can be handled by checking the relevant page, confirming the report link, or clarifying the type of issue. If a reader asks for a personalized interpretation outside the paid report flow, the answer may point them back to the tool, the guide library, or the report page. The contact channel is not a live divination service.

For delivery issues, the goal is practical: confirm whether checkout completed, whether a report link was generated, and whether the email address was typed correctly. For content corrections, the goal is editorial: improve clarity, remove internal wording, and make the page more useful to future visitors. For business inquiries, the site prefers products and partnerships that match cultural learning, books, cards, worksheets, or responsible digital reports.

Messages are reviewed with privacy in mind. Only send what is necessary to understand the issue. Keep personal details minimal, and never include passwords, payment credentials, or sensitive documents.

I Ching Reading Guide

A calm I Ching tool for hexagrams, changing lines, and reflective decisions.
